domestic.text: First-inning scoring probability drops 14% when both starters post a sub-3.00 ERA over their last 30 days.
Bullpen leverage index above 1.35 correlates with a 62% hold rate in high-pressure late-game situations.
Home teams batting .270+ against same-handed pitching convert RISP at 9% higher than league average.
Starting pitchers with a ground-ball rate above 52% reduce first-inning extra-base hits by 31%.
The model flags a 17% edge when a starter's chase rate exceeds 34% against a lineup with sub-25% O-swing.
NRFI probability climbs to 71% when both starters carry a first-inning ERA below 2.50 over their last 10 starts.
Park-adjusted HR factors above 1.15 inflate long-ball probability by 22% independent of pitcher profile.
Lineup-weighted OPS above .820 against a starter's primary pitch type signals a top-decile hitting environment.
Closers with a swinging-strike rate above 14% convert save opportunities at 88%, 11 points above average.
Back-end relievers with a walks-per-nine above 4.5 leak inherited runners at a 42% clip.
Pitchers allowing a barrel rate above 9% surrender HR at 1.6x the league baseline.
Stolen-base success rate exceeds 80% when the catcher's pop time is above 2.05 seconds.
Teams winning the first inning at home go on to win the game 64% of the time across all tracked seasons.
Platoon splits widen by 18% in day games — the model weights handedness more aggressively before sunset.
Starters on five or more days of rest show a 0.35 ERA reduction in the first three innings.
Umpire-specific strike zones shift NRFI probability by up to 8% at the extremes.
Weather-adjusted fly-ball distance increases 3.2 feet per 10°F above 75°F in non-dome parks.
Left-handed batters facing left-handed starters hit .217 — the model suppresses same-side prop projections accordingly.
The second time through the order, starters lose 12% of their strikeout rate on average.
Run-line value emerges at -1.5 when the implied moneyline probability exceeds 68%.
Pitchers with a curveball usage above 25% hold opponents to a .198 average through five innings.
Lineup turnover above 3 players from the previous game reduces team OPS by .025 on average.
Day-after-night-game fatigue depresses team wRC+ by 4.7% — a structural constant the model exploits.
First-inning YRFI probability exceeds 55% when both starters carry a WHIP above 1.40.
High-altitude parks inflate total bases projections by 11% — a non-negotiable model adjustment.
Catchers with a framing value above 1.5 runs per 200 pitches widen their starter's effective zone by 0.8 inches.
Batters facing a pitcher for the first time in a season carry a .040 OPS disadvantage.
The model identifies 23% more HR value when exit velocity exceeds 95 mph and launch angle sits between 25–30°.
Relief pitchers in their third consecutive appearance lose 1.1 mph average fastball velocity.
Team BABIP regresses to .300 over a 60-game window — short-term deviations are mean-reverting noise.
Starters with a K/BB ratio above 4.0 post a 2.95 expected ERA — elite command is the strongest constant.
Night-game humidity above 70% reduces fly-ball carry by 2.8 feet on average.
The model discounts saves from closers facing the bottom third of the order by 6% expected value.
Pitchers debuting a new pitch mid-season show a 15% whiff-rate spike in the first 50 batters faced.
Divisional matchups in the second half tighten run lines — the model contracts spreads by 0.3 runs.
Road underdogs with a top-10 bullpen cover the run line 54% of the time.
Starting pitchers with fewer than 5 innings in their last start regress 0.20 in next-game ERA.
Teams with a defensive runs saved above +10 reduce opponent BABIP by .012 — defense is not noise.
Pinch-hit batting average sits at .211 league-wide — cold bats are a structural truth.
The model adds 7% HR probability when wind blows out above 10 mph in open-air parks.
Starters facing a team for the third time in a season lose 19% of their strikeout edge.
Double-header second games depress total runs by 0.8 — fatigue is the market's blind spot.
Switch-hitters carry a .015 OPS advantage over platoon-locked hitters across full seasons.
Pitchers working on three days' rest lose 0.6 mph velocity and 8% of their swinging-strike rate.
Late-season call-ups batting below .220 in AAA project to a .195 major-league average.
The model's Bayesian prior for any given NRFI is 58.4% — everything else is an adjustment.
Starters pitching in their home park carry a 0.22 ERA advantage versus their road splits.
Batters with a hard-hit rate above 42% sustain power production independent of BABIP fluctuations.
Bullpen arms throwing fewer than 15 pitches in their last outing gain 0.4 mph on next appearance.
Lineup construction matters: leadoff hitters with an OBP above .360 increase team run expectancy by 0.3 per game.
Teams on winning streaks of 5+ see market overreaction — the model fades implied probability by 3%.
Ground-ball pitchers in wet conditions increase double-play rate by 14%.
The model tracks a 0.91 correlation between expected wOBA and actual offensive output over 200 PA.
Starters entering with a 10+ game quality-start streak maintain sub-3.00 ERA 78% of the time in the next outing.
Pitch-mix entropy above 1.8 bits correlates with a 22% increase in first-time-through deception.
Interleague games produce 0.4 more runs per game — unfamiliarity inflates scoring.
The model weights trailing bullpen matchups 40% heavier than pregame starting-pitcher analysis after the 6th inning.
Teams with a top-5 defensive WAR suppress opponent total bases by 6% compared to league median.
First-pitch strike percentage above 65% is the single strongest predictor of sub-1.20 WHIP.
Regression to the mean is not a theory — it is the only engine constant that never breaks.

overseas.text: KBO first-inning scoring frequency runs 8% higher than MLB — the model adjusts NRFI baselines accordingly.
NPB starters sustain effectiveness 0.7 innings deeper than MLB counterparts — fatigue curves diverge structurally.
KBO park factors range 30% wider than MLB — venue-specific adjustments are non-optional.
NPB batting averages cluster 12 points higher than MLB — contact-oriented leagues demand recalibrated baselines.
KBO bullpen usage patterns lag MLB trends by two years — leverage analysis requires local context.
NPB strikeout rates sit 15% below MLB — the model reduces whiff-dependent prop ceilings overseas.
KBO run environments average 1.2 runs per game higher than MLB — totals models diverge sharply.
NPB pitch counts are managed 20 pitches deeper per start — workload thresholds shift east.
KBO stolen-base attempts occur 40% more frequently — the model weights speed metrics heavier in Korea.
NPB umpire zones are 1.3 inches wider on average — pitcher-friendly constants dominate Japan.
KBO extra-inning rules accelerate scoring — the model treats overtime as a separate scoring environment.
NPB teams bunt 3.5x more than MLB — sacrifice strategies are structural, not situational.
KBO home-field advantage is 4% stronger than MLB — crowd energy is a measurable constant.
NPB relievers throw 70% fastballs — pitch-mix simplicity creates exploitable platoon splits.
KBO batting orders show less protection — cleanup hitters see 11% more pitches outside the zone.
NPB pitch velocity averages 2.3 mph slower than MLB — timing-based adjustments dominate projections.
KBO games average 18 minutes longer than MLB — deeper counts inflate pitch-dependent metrics.
NPB BABIP runs .015 higher than MLB — defensive range constants differ by league.
KBO pitchers with sub-50% ground-ball rates allow HR at 1.4x the NPB rate in comparable parks.
NPB batters facing foreign imports for the first time carry a .055 OPS deficit.
KBO day-game scoring rises 9% compared to night — the model tracks time-of-day constants by league.
NPB managers pull starters 22% earlier with a lead of 3+ runs — bullpen deployment is culturally driven.
KBO lineup depth drops sharply after the 6th spot — bottom-third production is 30% below MLB equivalent.
NPB walk rates sit 18% below MLB — free passes are rarer and more predictive when they occur.
KBO outfield dimensions average 5 meters shorter — fly-ball value is amplified structurally.
NPB postseason environments tighten ERA by 0.55 — October compression is universal.
KBO relief arms average 1.1 fewer pitches per plate appearance — efficiency shifts leverage calculations.
NPB hit-by-pitch rates are 35% higher than MLB — inside-pitch culture impacts baserunner projections.
KBO teams trailing after 5 innings win only 18% of the time — comeback probability is a structural floor.
NPB batting average on balls in play against left-handed pitching is .022 higher — platoon edges are wider.
KBO weather delays above 30 minutes depress second-half scoring by 7% — restart fatigue is real.
NPB pitchers with a slider usage above 30% hold opponents to .201 — the slider is king in Japan.
KBO first-pitch swing rate is 12% higher than MLB — aggressive early-count approaches reshape sequencing models.
NPB run expectancy per inning is 0.07 runs lower than MLB — tighter margins define the league.
KBO teams with a +20 run differential over the last 10 games cover spreads at 57%.
NPB catchers frame 1.8x more effectively than MLB average — pitch-call value is amplified.
KBO import pitchers carry a 0.45 ERA premium in their first month — adjustment periods are priced in.
NPB ground-ball rates are 6% higher than MLB — infield defense is weighted more heavily in projections.
KBO double-play rates exceed MLB by 11% — ground-ball sequencing has compounding value.
NPB pitchers with 100+ innings show 0.30 ERA regression in the second half — fatigue is deterministic.
KBO home-run rates in dome stadiums drop 14% versus open-air — environment is never neutral.
NPB interleague play produces 0.6 more runs per game — cross-league unfamiliarity is exploitable.
KBO starters with a WHIP below 1.15 post NRFI rates above 72% — elite command transcends leagues.
NPB sacrifice-fly rates are 25% higher than MLB — productive-out culture inflates run conversion.
KBO late-game substitution patterns add 0.4 runs of expected value per game — bench depth matters.
NPB road underdogs with top-5 pitching cover at 55% — the model trusts arms over lineups abroad.
KBO batters with 20+ HR show 0.035 ISO regression after the All-Star break — power fades faster.
NPB night-game temperatures below 15°C reduce fly-ball distance by 4.1 feet — cold suppresses power.
KBO middle-relief arms with fewer than 20 innings carry a 0.70 ERA inflation risk — sample size is everything.
NPB winning streaks above 6 games regress 61% of the time in the next series — momentum is a narrative.
KBO leadoff OBP above .380 generates 0.5 additional runs per game — table-setters are structural value.
NPB pitch-tunneling effectiveness is 20% higher due to lower velocity — deception outweighs raw stuff.
KBO teams playing 3+ games in 3 days see a 0.25 ERA rise across the pitching staff.
NPB starters on 6+ days' rest show a 0.40 ERA improvement in the first 4 innings — rest is universal.
KBO outfield-assist rates are 18% higher than MLB — arm strength and positioning are weighted constants.
NPB batting averages against breaking balls are .032 lower than against fastballs — pitch-type matchups dominate.
KBO clutch hitting with RISP outperforms MLB rates by 5% — small-ball execution compresses variance.
NPB game pace is 12 minutes slower than MLB — deliberate tempo impacts fatigue modeling.
KBO teams after a series sweep show a 7% letdown rate in the opener of the next series.
Cross-league constants converge at one truth: the model does not care about borders — only about edges.
